_comment: Deliberately-accepted registry findings — mirrors the pattern in workers/schema_drift_accepted.json. Every entry here is a gap the registry's mechanical checks would otherwise flag, confirmed by a concrete code/product audit to be intentional rather than a defect (e.g. a page that is correctly admin-only and therefore correctly unreachable from customer-facing navigation). Adding an entry here does not change the product; it only changes what future registry passes treat as expected rather than as new drift. Never add an entry here without a concrete reason tied to an actual audit — this file must not become a way to silence real gaps.
note: Population pending: 11 frontend pages were found unreachable from any navigation during the 2026-07-08 investigation (404.html expected; partner-portal.html already confirmed a real bug and fixed separately; the remaining 9 — billing-portal.html, copilot-admin.html, decision-dashboard.html, enterprise-dashboard.html, enterprise-kpi-dashboard.html, gadgets.html, ops-dashboard.html, revenue-intelligence-dashboard.html, security-fabric-dashboard.html — are unconfirmed and must not be assumed intentional. Each needs a dynamic_browser check (real click-through / access-control read) before being added here as an accepted exception; until then they remain open findings in the relevant domain registry entries, not silently excluded.
